error.signature: CROSSSLOT Keys in request don't hash to the same slot

verdict.summary: Multi-key command (MGET, MSET, pipeline, transaction) sent to Redis Cluster with keys in different hash slots. Cluster requires all keys in a multi-key operation to be in the same slot.

dead_ends.action: Disable cluster mode and use standalone Redis to avoid slot issues
dead_ends.why_fails: Loses all cluster benefits: high availability, horizontal scaling, automatic failover

dead_ends.action: Use separate single-key commands for every operation
dead_ends.why_fails: Replacing MGET with N GET commands increases latency by N round trips and loses atomicity

workarounds.action: Use hash tags to force related keys into the same slot
workarounds.how: Use {user:123}:profile and {user:123}:settings — the part in {} determines the slot

workarounds.action: Redesign data model to co-locate related keys
workarounds.how: Store related data in a single hash: HSET user:123 profile '...' settings '...'

workarounds.action: Use Lua scripting for atomic multi-key operations within the same slot
